critical.mandate: Every slide must have exactly one key message
Visual hierarchy drives attention — master it
Narrative arc must flow: hook, build, payoff

step.title: Audience and Purpose
step.action: Define the target audience: who are they, what do they know
Define the takeaway: what should the audience do, think, or feel after
Determine presentation context: live talk, async review, workshop

step.title: Content Inventory
step.action: Identify existing material: docs, data, images, research
Extract key data points, quotes, and evidence
Identify gaps — what content needs to be created

step.title: Narrative Arc
step.action: Define the story structure: hook (attention), build (evidence), payoff (action)
Create the throughline: one sentence that connects every slide
Plan emotional beats: surprise, tension, resolution

step.title: Slide Outline
step.action: One slide = one idea. Plan each slide with: title, key message, supporting visual
Include transition logic: how each slide connects to the next
Mark slides as: inform, persuade, transition, or evidence
Target: 1 slide per minute of talk time

step.title: Visual Design
step.action: Define color palette: 2-3 primary colors, 1 accent
Define typography: heading font, body font, sizes
Define layout patterns: title slide, content, comparison, data, quote
Specify consistency rules: margins, alignment, spacing

step.title: Generate Output
step.template-output: Generate slide deck specification with: audience brief, narrative arc, slide-by-slide content with speaker notes, visual design system, and layout specs.
